title: marrubium alysson var. plicatum
intro: Marrubium alysson var. plicatum, also known as Horehound, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) native to Europe and the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues such as indigestion and nausea, due to its anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. The health benefits of Marrubium alysson var. plicatum include its ability to reduce fever, soothe sore throats, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. The plant is typically prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
